steps.title: Identify the task requirement
steps.content: The task explicitly requires a generalization — meaning a broad conclusion about the overall effect of parks on happiness, not the methodology, sample scope, or specific city comparisons.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content: Choice B states that parks have an overall positive effect on happiness while varying by city, drawing from the average benefit of 0.10 (which exceeds zero) and data showing city-to-city variation. Choices A and D describe only the method and scope; choice C compares two specific cities and incorrectly claims Washington's parks had no positive effect when the data shows they did. This matches choice B — B is correct.
